details: Identifier and evidence quote match paper_key=164, but the source prints the boiling point as 139.1 ± 0.7 °C at 737.9 mm Hg while value_raw records only 139.1 °C.
DOI matches paper_key=164; Table 8.8 states chlorine dioxide has a boiling point of 11 °C, matching the row.
DOI matches paper_key=164; the glutaraldehyde discussion states melting and boiling points of -14 °C and 187 °C, respectively, matching the boiling-point row.
PMC identifier matches the paper directory; Section 3.1 compound PI-1 states 1-Ethoxyethyl-2-bromopropanoate has boiling point 87 °C at 3 mm Hg.
DOI and Table 3 match; K10/TMA support-activator gives Tm 133.7 °C, and the paper states polymer thermal properties were measured by DSC.
DOI and Table 3 match; K30/TEA support-activator gives Tm 134.5 °C, and the paper states polymer thermal properties were measured by DSC.
DOI and Table 3 match; K30/TMA support-activator gives Tm 134.0 °C, and the paper states polymer thermal properties were measured by DSC.
DOI matches the ACS Omega PDF; the nonisothermal DSC results assign the 223.4 °C higher melting peak to SC in the PLLA/PDLA (50/50) blend.
DOI matches the Pharmaceutics PDF; the DSC analysis states an endothermic peak onset at 164 °C assigned to melting of ITR.
DOI matches the Pharmaceutics PDF; the DSC analysis states the ITR-TER 1:2 systems have an onset around 320 °C assigned to excess TER.
